prompt: A field-notes client can edit a record while offline and reconnect through a replacement transport. Support sees two symptoms in the same reconnect trace: an edit sent just before the old transport vanished is never offered to the new transport, and a server snapshot briefly replaces an unsent local value even though the outbox still reports that mutation pending. Fresh online edits send and acknowledge normally, clean clients accept the same snapshots, and unrelated remote fields do update. Session identifiers are monotonic, acknowledgements are scoped to the session that sent the mutation, and server snapshots may omit keys created locally. Preserve mutation order and optimistic visibility; do not add timers, network calls, or persistence.
